get.summary: Read the channel's full action-permission matrix (defaults plus overrides).

put.summary: Override the required level for an action key (clamped to the action's floor).

delete.summary: Reset an action key's override back to its default required level.

get.summary: List all invite codes platform-wide, paginated.

post.summary: Mint an invite code with redemption cap, optional tier/founders-badge grant, and expiry.
Revoke an invite code, blocking further redemptions.
Manually grant a channel a tier without Stripe, optionally marked as an invite-only grant.
Grant a channel the founders badge directly.

get.summary: Returns aggregate statistics for the admin dashboard.
Returns all channels with their current status.
Returns all registered users.
Returns system health and process metrics.
Returns service health list (for dashboard health panel).
Returns recent platform events for the admin dashboard.
Read the channel's daily analytics series for a date range.
Read the channel's aggregated analytics summary for a date range.
Rank the channel's top viewers by the chosen metric over a date range.
List viewer analytics profiles matching the query, paginated.
Read a single viewer's analytics profile (self-or-Gate-2, authorized in-action).
Read a viewer's engagement series over a date range (self-or-Gate-2).
Read a viewer's current attendance streak (self-or-Gate-2).

post.summary: Set or clear a viewer's analytics opt-out flag (self-or-Gate-2).

get.summary: Get the currently authenticated user.

Start the Twitch OAuth flow. Redirects the browser to Twitch's authorization page.
Pass `redirect_uri` for mobile / desktop-loopback deep-link callbacks (e.g.
`nomnomzbot://callback`). Pass `client=web` for the served-web dashboard: a full-page redirect
can't receive a JSON token body, so the callback returns the tokens in the URL fragment + an HttpOnly
cookie instead.

Handle the OAuth callback from Twitch. Exchanges the authorization code for
platform tokens. If a mobile `redirect_uri` was passed in the original
request, the browser is redirected to the app's deep link with tokens in the
query string. Otherwise returns a JSON response (for web clients).

post.summary:
Exchange an OAuth authorization code for platform tokens (mobile / SPA flow).
The client handles the Twitch redirect directly and sends the code + redirect_uri
to this endpoint for server-side token exchange.

Begin the no-secret streamer login (Device Code Flow). Returns a short user code + the
twitch.tv/activate URL to show the operator, plus the device code the client polls with. No Twitch app
registration and no client secret — NomNomzBot's shipped public client id drives it.

Poll a streamer device login once. Until the operator approves, the status is `pending` /
`slow_down`; on `authorized` the session is opened and the response carries the platform
tokens + user, which the client establishes exactly as it would after the redirect callback.
Refresh an expired access token.
Log out the current session, revoking its refresh tokens.
Log out of all the current user's sessions, revoking every refresh token they hold.

get.summary:
Start the OAuth flow for the platform-shared bot account. This is a platform-level registration on top
of the operator's account (not a login), so it is Plane-C gated (identity-auth §5,
`platform · iam:manage` — the policy name is the IAM permission key verbatim, audited on SaaS).
Returns the Twitch authorize URL for the client to open — the resulting token is stored globally (no
per-channel binding). First-run setup uses the wizard's `system/setup/bot/oauth-url` instead
(before an admin exists).

delete.summary: Disconnect the platform-shared bot account, revoking its Twitch token (platform-operator only).

get.summary: Get the current platform-shared bot account connection status (platform-operator only).

post.summary:
Begin the bot-account device login (no secret). The operator approves the bot's own Twitch account at
twitch.tv/activate and the client polls until connected. Admin-gated — the streamer is already signed in
when they add a bot account (Streamer.bot parity).
Poll a bot device login once; on `authorized` the shared bot account is connected + vaulted.

get.summary: Get the channel's current subscription state.
List the publicly offered billing tiers (channel-independent catalogue).
Get the channel's resolved entitlement — the effective features and limits of its tier.
Get the channel's current metered usage across billed metrics.
List the channel's invoices, paginated.

post.summary: Start a checkout session for the requested tier, returning the provider's checkout URL.
Change the channel's subscription to another tier, immediately or at period end.
Cancel the channel's subscription, immediately or at period end.
Resume the channel's subscription, undoing a pending cancellation.
Create a billing-provider portal session for self-serve payment management.

get.summary: Get the channel's founders-badge status.

post.summary: Validate an invite code without redeeming it.
Redeem an invite code for the channel, applying its tier and/or founders-badge grant.
Receive a Stripe webhook event, passing the raw payload and Stripe-Signature header to the handler for HMAC verification and dispatch.

get.summary: Lists all built-in commands for the channel, with their enabled state and defaults.

post.summary: Purchase a catalog item as the authenticated caller, with the buyer's role level resolved server-side.

get.summary: List the channel's catalog purchases, filtered and paginated.

post.summary: Refund a purchase, binding the acting refunder to the authenticated caller.

get.summary: Returns OAuth scopes status for the broadcaster token on this channel.

Start Twitch OAuth for this channel's white-label bot — an additional bot-identity registration on top
of the owner's account (not a login), handled like an integration connect. Gated owner-level
(identity-auth §5, `management/Broadcaster · channelbot:connect`). Returns the Twitch authorize URL
(force_verify) for the client to open; the callback completes via the unified, nonce-validated
`/auth/twitch/callback`.
Get white-label bot status for a specific channel.

delete.summary: Disconnect the white-label bot for a specific channel.

get.summary: List the channel's federation opt-ins.

put.summary: Create or update a federation opt-in for the channel, attributed to the acting caller.

delete.summary: Disable a federation opt-in, returning that flow to default-deny.

get.summary: List all channels the current user owns or moderates.

post.summary:
Onboards the caller's channel. Thin by design: Task<Result<ChannelDto>> IChannelService.OnboardAsync(string broadcasterId, CreateChannelRequest request, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default(CancellationToken)) owns every
side effect, including publishing `ChannelOnboardedEvent` — the single onboarding path that fans
out to the auto-discovered seed handlers (rewards, mods/VIPs/subs, channel info, owner profile, event
responses, banned-user import, bot mod-join, default builtin commands, EventSub subscribe). The former
inline Twitch-data sync that used to live here has moved into those handlers, matching every other
controller's validate → service → response shape.

get.summary: Get all Twitch channels the current user moderates (from Twitch API, not just DB).
Retrieve a channel by ID — dashboard channel info, Moderator-floored like every dashboard read.

delete.summary: Delete a channel and all its associated data.

post.summary: Bot joins the channel (subscribes to EventSub and starts listening).
Bot leaves the channel (unsubscribes from EventSub).
Reset all channel bot configuration to defaults (clears Configuration entries).

Read the channel's currency configuration.

get.summary: List the channel's currency accounts (wallets), paginated.

The caller's OWN wallet on this channel — the `self` arm of economy.md §5's
`economy:account:read` "self-or-Gate-2". It binds the subject to the authenticated caller and can
only ever return that caller's account, so it carries no Gate-2 floor (community / Everyone): a
participant reads their own balance here without the Moderator `economy:account:read` the keyed
`accounts/{viewerUserId}` route demands. `me` is a literal, so it never shadows the guid route.
Read a viewer's wallet by user id, creating the account on first access (the Gate-2 arm of self-or-Gate-2).
Page through a viewer's currency ledger entries.

post.summary: Admin-adjust a viewer's balance, binding the subject to the route and the actor to the authenticated caller.
Freeze or unfreeze a viewer's wallet.
Transfer currency between viewers, binding the acting sender to the authenticated caller.

get.summary: List a guild connection's notification dispatch log, paginated.

Start the Discord OAuth flow — redirects to Discord's authorization page with bot + guilds scopes.
Pass redirect_uri for the desktop client's loopback listener (RFC-8252): the callback
then redirects there with a success/error marker so the listener completes. The web/SPA client omits it
and gets the frontend redirect instead.

Handle the Discord OAuth callback. Exchanges the authorization code for tokens, parses the response into
a DiscordGuildOAuthResult, and delegates persistence to IDiscordGuildService
(which vaults the bot token and records the guild link). No persistence is done inline.
List the channel's leaderboard configurations.

delete.summary: Delete a leaderboard configuration by id.

get.summary: List the channel's configured event responses, paginated, for the dashboard's event responses page.
Get the configured response for a single event type.

put.summary: Create or replace the response configured for an event type.

delete.summary: Delete the configured response for an event type.

post.summary: Exports the channel's whole journal as a JSONL file download (one event envelope per line). Owner-only.

Imports a JSONL journal export into the channel's stream — idempotent (duplicates by EventId are skipped),
upcast on read, and atomic (all-or-nothing). Owner-only. Returns import/skip/upcast counts.

Backfills the channel's history from the legacy NoMercy bot database into the journal — reads the legacy file
READ-ONLY, maps each channel event to its current domain event, and appends idempotently (a re-run imports
nothing). Owner-only. Returns import/skip counts and the journal head before/after.

Rebuilds every tenant-scoped read-model projection (channel event log, analytics dailies, viewer profiles, …)
from the channel's journal — reset → replay from position 0. Owner-level. Run after a legacy import (or any
journal backfill) so the new facts surface in the read models. Returns the per-projection applied-event counts.

get.summary: The integrations screen read model: per provider — connected?, account, granted scope-sets, capabilities.

post.summary:
Starts a provider connect: returns the authorize URL the client opens (the service builds the PKCE
challenge + signed single-use state). A connect carries a progressive scope-set key (e.g.
`spotify.playback`); re-connecting with a wider set is an incremental re-auth.

get.summary:
The provider redirect target. Anonymous — secured by the signed single-use `state`, not the JWT.
Validates+consumes state, exchanges the code (with the PKCE verifier) for tokens, vaults them, then
redirects the browser back to the connect's return URL. A failure redirects to the integrations screen
with an error marker rather than surfacing a raw API error to the browser.

post.summary: Severs a provider connection: revokes the token where supported, then soft-deletes + crypto-shreds the vault entry. Idempotent.

get.summary: List all available integrations and their connection state for the channel.

delete.summary: Disconnect an external integration (revokes tokens, removes connection state).

get.summary: List active and ended polls for the channel.

post.summary: Create a new poll with optional channel point voting.

patch.summary: End a poll with a specified outcome (ARCHIVED or TERMINATED).

get.summary: List active and ended predictions for the channel.

post.summary: Create a new channel prediction with multiple outcomes.

patch.summary: End a prediction with RESOLVED or CANCELED status and optional winning outcome.

post.summary: Start a raid to another channel.

delete.summary: Cancel a raid that has not yet begun.

Delay the next scheduled ad break by 8 minutes.

List the channel's management-role memberships, paginated (flat PaginatedResponse shape).

put.summary: Assign or change a user's management role on the channel, recorded as a bot grant.

delete.summary: Remove a user's management role from the channel.

get.summary:
The authenticated caller's own resolved access on this channel (roles-permissions §5.1). The shell calls
this on session establish to learn the caller's effective ManagementRole and drive the
role-correct sidebar + write affordances. Self-introspection, so it is gated by entry (Gate 1) only — a
pure viewer with no management role must be able to learn they have none (and so be routed to the
participation-only surface), which a `roles:read` (Moderator) floor would forbid.

A channel member's resolved access (roles-permissions §5.1). Floors at `roles:read` (Moderator) —
inspecting another user's access is a management read; Task<IActionResult> RolesController.EffectiveMe(string channelId, CancellationToken ct) is the unfloored
self-introspection sibling.
List the savings jars this channel is a member of.

post.summary: Invite another channel into the jar, binding the jar id from the route.

put.summary:
Update the channel's stream metadata (title, game, tags) in a single call. Carries the same Editor
floor as the granular PATCH routes (`channel:title:write`) so a Moderator cannot use this combined
route to bypass the per-field `channel:*:write` gates (stream-admin §5; all three fields floor at
Editor).

patch.summary: Update stream game/category (with Twitch search resolution).

get.summary: Search Twitch game categories (for autocomplete).
Returns system readiness status. Anonymous — must be callable before any user can log in.

The self-describing onboarding wizard: the ordered steps, each with its copy, step-by-step instructions, the
exact redirect URI to register, the API call that satisfies it, the input fields, and its live completion
state. A dashboard renders the entire first-time-setup flow from this single call.
Get the OAuth URL to authorize the platform bot account.
Check the platform bot connection status.

put.summary: Save system-level Twitch app credentials.
Save system-level Spotify app credentials.
Save system-level Discord app credentials.
Save system-level YouTube (Google) app credentials.

post.summary:
Finalize first-run setup. Once called — or once the system is otherwise ready — the credential
endpoints lock to platform admins, so anonymous callers can no longer repoint the platform's app
credentials. Callable anonymously only while setup is still open (the first-run window).

get.summary:
Returns the full pronoun catalogue. Anonymous — the participant Me screen shows pronouns before login
and allows the user to pick their preferred pronoun without requiring authentication.
List the channel's timers, paginated.

post.summary: Create a new scheduled timer for the channel.

get.summary: Get a single timer by id.

put.summary: Update an existing timer's schedule, message, or enabled state.

delete.summary: Delete a timer.

post.summary: Enable or disable a timer without deleting it.

get.summary: Get the channel's TTS configuration.

put.summary: Update the channel's TTS configuration.

get.summary: List the voices available from the configured TTS provider.

post.summary: Generate a short test TTS clip to preview a voice.

get.summary:
The current channel's Twitch scope/connection matrix: connection status, granted scopes, and the
per-feature granted/missing requirement rows. `404` when this channel has no Twitch connection.

The channel's outstanding Twitch scope gaps — the deduplicated set of scopes the streamer token is missing
(proactive feature-gated gaps ∪ reactive gaps a real Helix call surfaced), each with the feature(s) it
blocks and whether the streamer was already told in chat. The dashboard renders the "grant '<scope>'"
banner from this. `404` when this channel has no Twitch connection.

post.summary:
Start the one-click additive scope re-grant: a secret-free streamer Device Code Flow requesting
`granted ∪ missing`, so the operator re-consents to the full set and the existing grant is never
dropped. The client shows the user code + verification URL and polls the normal streamer device poll
(`POST /auth/twitch/device/poll`); on approval the widened grant reconciles onto the connection and the
gaps clear. `404` when there is no Twitch connection; `409` when nothing is missing.

get.summary: Search for users by name or username.
Retrieve a user by ID (self-or-Gate-2, authorized in-action).
Retrieve a user's profile information (self-or-Gate-2, authorized in-action).

put.summary: Update a user's profile information — strictly the user themselves (or a platform admin).

get.summary: Get all channels a user appears in (as broadcaster or moderation role).
Returns a summary of the user's data (GDPR data summary).

Export all personal data for the specified user (GDPR right of access).
Returns a JSON file download containing all data we hold for this user.

delete.summary:
Delete all personal data for the specified user (GDPR right to erasure).
This action is irreversible.

get.summary: List the channel's inbound webhook endpoints, paginated.

delete.summary: Delete an inbound webhook endpoint, retiring its ingest URL.

post.summary: Rotate the opaque URL token for an inbound webhook endpoint, invalidating the old ingest URL.

get.summary: List the channel's outbound webhook endpoints, paginated.

post.summary: Create an outbound webhook endpoint for the channel, recording the caller as its creator.

get.summary: Fetch a single outbound webhook endpoint by id.

put.summary: Update an outbound webhook endpoint's configuration.

delete.summary: Delete an outbound webhook endpoint, stopping its deliveries.

post.summary: Rotate the shared secret used to sign an outbound webhook endpoint's deliveries.
Re-enable an outbound webhook endpoint that was disabled after delivery failures.
Send a test delivery to an outbound webhook endpoint.

get.summary: List a channel's overlay widgets, paginated.

post.summary: Create a new overlay widget for a channel.

get.summary: Get a single overlay widget's configuration.

put.summary: Update an existing overlay widget's configuration.

delete.summary: Delete an overlay widget from a channel.

AddQuoteRequest.description: Create payload for a new quote (quotes.md §3). `QuotedAt` defaults to creation when null.

AdminAdjustCommand.description: A broadcaster/admin manual credit or debit (economy.md §4). Sign of long AdminAdjustCommand.Amount decides direction.

AuthResultDto.description: Authentication result containing tokens and user info.

BotStatusDto.description: DTO describing the connected bot account.

CancelSubscriptionRequest.description: Cancel the subscription (immediate or at period end).

ChannelDto.description: Full channel detail for dashboard views.

ChannelMembershipDto.description: A channel-management membership row for the dashboard roles screen (roles-permissions §4).

ChatBadgeDto.description:
A chat badge (subscriber, moderator, etc.) resolved to its scale-keyed image urls ("1"/"2"/"4") from the cached
Helix badge sets (chat-decoration spec §3.3). `Urls` is empty when the badge set is not yet warmed in cache.

ChatCheermoteDto.description:
Cheermote fragment data — the raw prefix/bits/tier plus the resolved tier image (chat-decoration spec §3.4):
scale-keyed `Urls`, whether animated, and the tier `ColorHex`. The image fields are null/empty until the
channel's cheermotes are warmed in cache.

ChatEmoteDto.description:
Emote fragment data — the unified, render-ready shape covering Twitch and third-party (BTTV/FFZ/7TV) emotes
(chat-decoration spec §4). `Urls` is scale-keyed ("1"/"2"/"3"); `Provider` is the source network.

ChatFragmentDto.description: A single fragment of a chat message: text | emote | cheermote | mention | link.

ChatLinkPreviewDto.description: OpenGraph link-preview data (chat-decoration spec §3.5); fields are null when the page exposes no tags.

ChatMentionDto.description:
Mention fragment data (@user) plus the mentioned user's last-seen chat colour as #RRGGBB (chat-decoration spec §3.1),
or null when that user has not been seen recently.

CheckItem.description: One readiness check. bool CheckItem.Ready means the area is usable now; bool CheckItem.Ok means the full credential set is present. For Twitch the two diverge: a client id alone makes the bot fully functional via the secret-free Device Code Flow (bool CheckItem.Ready = true), while a client secret is the pure enhancement that also unlocks the one-tap redirect sign-in (bool CheckItem.Ok = true). For every other area the two coincide. The frontend routes onboarding off these: bool CheckItem.Ready gates whether the flow can start at all; bool CheckItem.Ok picks the smoother redirect login over device-code.

CommandListItem.description:
Lightweight command info for list views — includes the response text and pipeline id so the edit
            form can pre-fill without a separate detail fetch.

ConnectIntegrationRequest.description: The connect request body: the progressive scope-set to request, and where to return after connect (integrations-oauth §5).

CreateChannelRequest.description: Request to create/onboard a new channel.

CreateCodeScriptRequest.description: Create a named script + its Version 1 (custom-code.md §4). No BroadcasterId (broker invariant).

CreateCodeScriptVersionRequest.description: Append a new immutable version; `Publish` = save-and-swap on valid (custom-code.md §4).

CreateCommandDto.description: Request to create a new command.

CreateEventSubSubscriptionRequest.description: The POST body for creating one subscription (twitch-eventsub §4.4).

verificationSecret.description: The provider token / shared secret. AEAD-encrypted on store, never persisted in plaintext.

fqdn.description: Must match an enabled HttpEgressAllowlist (H.7) row for the tenant.

CreatePipelineDto.description: Request to create a new pipeline.

CreateTimerDto.description: Request to create a new timer.

CurrentUserDto.description: The currently authenticated user, as surfaced to the dashboard.

CustomDataSourceDto.description: Safe read projection — the `AuthSecret` is never echoed; only its presence is surfaced.

viewerCount.description: Last known viewer count (0 if offline or not yet received from Twitch).

followerCount.description: Follower count (not tracked server-side; always 0 in this implementation).

uptime.description: Stream uptime in whole seconds, null if offline.

DashboardStatsDto.description:
Snapshot of a channel's current state, returned by GET /api/v1/dashboard/{broadcasterId}/stats.
Live fields come from the in-memory ChannelContext; counts are session totals since bot join.

DeviceBotPollDto.description: One bot device-login poll: the loop string DeviceBotPollDto.Status, plus the bot connection on "authorized".

DeviceCodeStartDto.description:
A started Device Code Flow authorization (the no-secret login). The client shows string DeviceCodeStartDto.UserCode and
sends the operator to string DeviceCodeStartDto.VerificationUri, then polls with string DeviceCodeStartDto.DeviceCode every
int DeviceCodeStartDto.Interval seconds until int DeviceCodeStartDto.ExpiresIn elapses.

DeviceLoginPollDto.description: One streamer device-login poll: the loop string DeviceLoginPollDto.Status, plus the issued auth on "authorized".

DevicePollRequest.description: The body of a device-login poll request: the opaque device code from the start call.

DiscordEmbedDto.description: The `[VC:JSON]` embed shape — mirrors the Domain `DiscordEmbedConfig` on the wire.

DiscordGuildChannelDto.description: A live guild channel, for the target/button channel pickers. `Type` is Discord's channel type (0 = text).

DiscordGuildConnectionDto.description: Read model of a Discord guild link (discord.md §4). Ids are `Guid`; Discord ids are `string`.

DiscordGuildInfoDto.description: The linked guild's live profile, proxied from Discord for the dashboard.

DiscordGuildRoleDto.description: A live guild role, for the notify-role picker. `Managed` roles cannot be self-assigned.

EarningRuleDto.description: One channel earning rule (economy.md §4). `Source` is an `EarningSource` token.

EditQuoteRequest.description: Edit payload (quotes.md §3). The per-channel `Number` is immutable and not editable.

EntitlementDto.description: A tenant's effective entitlement — the single source feature-gating + quota checks read.

EventJournalImportSummary.description:
The outcome of an import: how many lines the file held, how many events were newly appended, how many were
skipped as already-present duplicates (idempotency), and how many were upcast from a stale shape on the way in.

EventSubReconcileReportDto.description: The outcome of a registry reconcile pass (twitch-eventsub §4.4).

EventSubSubscriptionDto.description: One registry row projected to the controller surface (twitch-eventsub §4.4).

FeatureStatusDto.description: The enablement status of a single channel feature.

GrantAgeConsentRequest.description: Grant a viewer's explicit 18+ gambling consent (economy.md §4).

GrantTierRequest.description: Admin-grant a tier to a channel without Stripe.

InboundWebhookEndpointDto.description: An inbound webhook endpoint view (webhooks.md §4). Never exposes the verification secret — only a set/unset flag.

IntegrationStatusDto.description: Per-provider status for the integrations screen (integrations-oauth §4). No secrets.

InviteChannelRequest.description: Invite a partner channel into a jar (economy.md §4). string InviteChannelRequest.Role is a JarRole token.

InviteCodeRequest.description: Validate or redeem an invite code by its code string.

InviteCodeValidationDto.description: The result of validating an invite code (pre-redemption).

JarContributeRequest.description: Contribute a viewer's currency into a jar (economy.md §4). long JarContributeRequest.Amount > 0.

JarWithdrawRequest.description: Withdraw from a jar back to a viewer's account (economy.md §4). long JarWithdrawRequest.Amount > 0.

LegacyImportResult.description:
The outcome of a legacy import, plus the journal head before/after so the caller can see the growth.
IReadOnlyDictionary<string, long> LegacyImportResult.SkippedByLegacyType itemizes the unmapped skips by their legacy `Type` so the operator can
verify every legacy row is accounted for (imported + duplicate + itemized skips = total read), with no silent drop.

MissingScopeDto.description:
One missing-scope row: the absent scope, the feature(s) it blocks (human-grouped by the
`FeatureScopeMap` key), whether a real Helix call already failed for it (bool MissingScopeDto.DetectedAtRuntime),
and whether the streamer has already been told in chat (bool MissingScopeDto.ChatNotified). The dashboard renders
"the bot needs '<scope>' to <features>" from this and the [Grant permission] button re-grants the union.

MissingScopesDto.description:
The channel's outstanding Twitch scope gaps (identity-auth §3.4a) — the read model the dashboard banner
renders and the one-click re-grant acts on. IReadOnlyList<MissingScopeDto> MissingScopesDto.Scopes is the deduplicated set of scopes the
streamer token is missing (proactive feature-gated gaps the connection never held + reactive gaps a real
Helix call surfaced); empty when the connection holds everything every offered feature needs.

MusicQueueDto.description: Full music queue including now playing and upcoming tracks.

redirectUri.description:
Custom redirect URI used by the client (e.g. `nomnomzbot://callback` for mobile).
When provided, Twitch token exchange uses this instead of the server's configured URI.

OAuthCallbackDto.description: OAuth callback data received from the Twitch redirect.

OAuthStartDto.description: The authorize URL the client opens plus the signed single-use state (integrations-oauth §4).

PipelineDto.description: Full pipeline details including the deserialized node graph cache.

PipelineListItemDto.description: Lightweight pipeline summary for list views.

PlayGameRequest.description: Play a game (economy.md §4). int PlayGameRequest.RoleLevel is the caller's resolved community level.

ProjectionRebuildResult.description: How many events one named projection applied during a rebuild.

PronounCatalogDto.description: Catalog entry as returned by the public catalog endpoint.

QueueItemDto.description: A queue item with its position.

QuoteDto.description: The transport/read shape of a quote (quotes.md §3).

RedeemInviteCodeResultDto.description: The result of redeeming an invite code.

RedemptionListItem.description:
One entry in the channel-points redemption queue (rewards.md) — a redemption with its current
            status (`unfulfilled` / `fulfilled` / `canceled`), folded from the journal.

RefreshTokenRequest.description:
Token refresh request. The refresh token is optional in the body: the served-web dashboard sends none and
relies on the HttpOnly cookie the browser attaches automatically, while native clients send the token they
hold in their own vault.

ScopeRegrantStartDto.description:
A started re-grant device authorization (identity-auth §3.4a): the same secret-free Device Code Flow the
streamer login uses, but requesting `granted ∪ missing` so the existing grant is never dropped. The
client shows string ScopeRegrantStartDto.UserCode at string ScopeRegrantStartDto.VerificationUri and polls the normal streamer device
poll with string ScopeRegrantStartDto.DeviceCode; on approval the token store reconciles the new scopes and the gap clears.

SetAnalyticsOptOutRequest.description: Body for the viewer analytics opt-out toggle (analytics.md §5).

SetFeatureFlagOverrideRequest.description: Set a per-tenant override (the internal/beta opt-in or per-channel kill-switch).

SetFeatureFlagRequest.description: Create-or-update a flag's global definition (rollout-updates §5 ramp controls).

pronounId.description: The int Pronoun.Id to set as primary, or `null` to leave unchanged, or `0` to clear.

altPronounId.description: The int Pronoun.Id to set as alt, or `null` to leave unchanged, or `0` to clear.

manualOverride.description: When `true`, pin this choice and prevent automatic provider resolution from overwriting it.

SetPronounRequest.description: Request to set or clear pronouns for the authenticated viewer.

SetupActionDto.description:
How a UI completes a step. `Type` is `save_credentials` (POST/PUT the IReadOnlyList<SetupFieldDto> SetupStepDto.Fields
to `Endpoint`) or `oauth_redirect` (GET `Endpoint` for a URL to send the browser to, then poll
`PollEndpoint` for completion).

SetupFieldDto.description: One input the user fills in for a step (key, label, control type, required flag, help text).

SetupStepDto.description: One onboarding step: presentation + live state + the action and fields that complete it.

SetupWizardDto.description:
The self-describing first-time-setup contract a dashboard renders the onboarding flow from: an ordered list of
steps plus an overall bool SetupWizardDto.Complete flag. Each step carries everything the UI needs — copy, the exact
redirect URI to register, the API call that satisfies it, the input fields, and its live completion state — so
the onboarding pages need no hardcoded knowledge of the steps.

SongRequestDto.description: Request to add a song to the queue.

StartCheckoutRequest.description: Start a Stripe Checkout for a tier.

TierDto.description: A public billing plan with its limits.

TierLimitDto.description: One quota lever for a tier (`-1` = unlimited).

TimerListItem.description: Lightweight timer info for list views.

TopViewerMetric.description: The metric a top-viewers query ranks by (analytics.md §4).

TransferCommand.description: A point-to-point transfer between two viewers in the same channel (economy.md §4). long TransferCommand.Amount > 0.

TtsTestRequestDto.description: Request to test a TTS voice.

TtsTestResultDto.description: Result of a TTS test synthesis.

TwitchAdSchedule.description:
The channel's ad schedule — next/last ad as unix seconds (0 when none), the current ad duration, the
remaining pre-roll-free time, and the snooze allowance (count plus the unix refresh time).

TwitchAdSnooze.description: Result of snoozing the next ad — the updated snooze count, refresh time, and pushed-back next-ad time (unix seconds).

TwitchClipStub.description:
The stub returned by a clip-create call (Create Clip, Create Clip From VOD): the new clip's id and the
edit URL the broadcaster opens to trim and publish it within the 15-second editing window.

TwitchCommercial.description: Result of starting a commercial — the length served, a status message, and the retry cooldown.

TwitchPoll.description: A poll the broadcaster created — its choices, voting settings, status and lifecycle timestamps.

TwitchPollChoice.description: One choice in a poll, with its running vote tallies (Get/Create/End Polls).

TwitchPredictionOutcome.description: One of the 2–10 possible outcomes of a prediction, with its running tally of predictors.

TwitchPredictionTopPredictor.description: A leading predictor on an outcome — points wagered and (once resolved) points won.

TwitchRaid.description: The pending raid created by Start a Raid — when it was created and whether the target is mature.

TwitchScopeDiagnosticsDto.description:
The Twitch scope/connection health read model (twitch-helix.md §5): the connection status, the granted
scope set, and the per-feature requirement matrix the dashboard renders so a missing scope is observable
(it closes the "subscriber count always 0" / "403 chat" class of silent failures by surfacing the gap).

TwitchScopeRequirementDto.description:
One row of the diagnostics matrix: a scope a feature needs, whether the connection currently holds it, and
(because every entry here is a progressive, feature-gated scope) the feature that gates its request. A
missing progressive scope is "feature-gated", not an error — it is requested when the feature is enabled.

UpdateCatalogItemRequest.description: Partial update of a catalog item (PATCH semantics — null = unchanged) (economy.md §4).

UpdateCommandDto.description: Request to update an existing command.

UpdateEventResponseDto.description: Request to update an event response configuration.

verificationSecret.description: When present, rotates (re-encrypts) the verification secret.

UpdatePipelineDto.description: Request to update an existing pipeline.

UpdateTimerDto.description: Request to update an existing timer.

pronounId.description: The int Pronoun.Id to assign, or `null` to leave unchanged, or `0` to clear.

UpdateUserProfileRequest.description: Request to update a user profile.

UpsertEarningRuleRequest.description: Upsert one earning rule (economy.md §4). string UpsertEarningRuleRequest.Source is an `EarningSource` token.

UpsertGameConfigRequest.description: Upsert a game configuration (economy.md §4). string UpsertGameConfigRequest.Category is a GameCategory token.

UpsertLeaderboardConfigRequest.description: Create or update a leaderboard config (economy.md §4). Guid? UpsertLeaderboardConfigRequest.Id null = create.

UsageMetricDto.description: Current-period usage for one metered key vs the tier limit.

UserChannelAppearanceDto.description: Channel appearance record for GDPR user data view.

UserPronounDto.description: Pronoun state for the self-service API.

UserStatsDto.description: GDPR data summary for a user.

ViewerProfileSort.description: How the viewer list is sorted (analytics.md §4).
